raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing this complaint because my credit card issuer, Citi, has failed to properly resolve a clear and documented case of merchant fraud. 

On XX/XX/year>, I was charged {$19000.00} by a seller calling itself XXXX XXXX XXXX. After payment, the merchant never shipped any goods, stopped responding, and was discovered to be using a completely fake identity. 

The merchant provided : A fake business address ( XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX, NY no such business exists there ) A XXXX  phone number not tied to any legitimate company A fabricated invoice using a non-existent company name A website ( XXXX ) not registered to any legitimate XXXX entity A XXXX page created and operated under false pretenses After taking my payment, the merchant disappeared and provided no tracking number, no shipment, and no resolution. 

I immediately contacted Citi to dispute the charge. However, Citi has not issued a credit and has not properly classified this as fraud despite overwhelming evidence. This is a violation of the Fair Credit Billing Act regarding : Goods not received Merchant identity misrepresentation Unauthorized or fraudulent transactions I am requesting the CFPB compel Citi to : 1. Reclassify the dispute as fraud.

2. Issue a full reversal of the charge.

3. Provide a written explanation of corrective action. 

I am attaching all evidence, including : Fake invoice Screenshots of communication Proof of cancellation Proof of no shipment Evidence that the merchant identity is fraudulent Thank you for your assistance.
